title: All the news about Anthropic’s new AI fight with the White House
slides: Anthropic just escalated its White House standoff—why?
The company refused a new executive order on AI safety.
Their model passed tests that others publicly failed.
Your compliance team might now lack legal cover.
Re-audit your AI supply chain for political exposure.
thread_text:
1. Why Anthropic said no.

Refusing a White House executive order on AI safety, the company bet its model passed tests others failed.

2. The test they passed.

Their model aced safety benchmarks that rivals tripped on publicly. One detail: the government’s own red team couldn’t break it.

3. Compliance gap exposed.

Your legal team relied on the order as a shield. Without it, third-party audits carry political risk you can’t insure against.

4. Supply chain blind spot.

Your vendors might certify against the skipped standard. If they’re exposed, your contracts have no escape clause for political fallout.

5. Audit your AI chain now.

Map every model your team touches. If any relied on that executive order for approval, simulate a cutoff—can your product survive without it?
video_script: Anthropic refused an AI safety executive order.

Political exposure may break your contracts.

instagram.caption:
Your compliance team just lost their legal shield. Anthropic refused a White House executive order on AI safety, betting their model could pass tests others failed.

The government’s own red team couldn’t break Anthropic’s model. But that same order was what your contracts relied on for regulatory cover. 🛡️ If your vendors certified against a standard the White House just escalated, your supply chain now carries political risk you can’t insure against.

Map every model your team touches today—can your product survive without that executive order as a fallback? ⚖️
